content: Language education's vocabulary learning of the term 'reflexion' that embeds the practice of active reflection on the word's use and context can improve the performance of LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by grounding abstract self-improvement routines in the same embodied associative memory building that human learners use when mastering the concept itself. This framing turns the act of teaching the linguistic concept of reflection into a training method for LLMs to build more robust self-reflection capabilities.
